name: obsidian-Smart2Brain
description: An Obsidian plugin to interact with your privacy focused AI-Assistant making your second brain even smarter!

commit.message:
feat: Implement privacy management features (#268)

* feat: Implement privacy management features

- Added Privacy Warning Modal to alert users when switching to untrusted providers with private notes in chat history.
- Introduced Privacy List Modal for managing files considered private, allowing users to add or remove entries and toggle between exclusion and inclusion modes.
- Enhanced chat history checks for private notes, ensuring sensitive data is not processed by untrusted providers.
- Updated ProviderItem to include a toggle for marking providers as trusted for private data.
- Implemented indexing report functionality to provide insights on indexed and skipped files during the embedding process.
- Added support for handling skipped files with detailed reasons during indexing, improving user awareness of potential issues.

* fix: Enhance privacy management by adding 'not-indexed' reason and updating UI components

feat: enhance UMAP options

- Updated `pdfExtractor.ts` to improve text extraction from PDFs.
- Introduced `UMAPOptions` interface in `projection.ts` to allow customization of UMAP parameters such as `nNeighbors` and `minDist`.
- Modified `reduceDimensions` and `umapReduce` functions to accept UMAP options.
- Added new settings for UMAP neighbors and minimum distance in `GraphSettings.svelte`.
- Updated `SmartGraphView` and `graphDataBuilder` to utilize new UMAP settings.
- Enhanced tests to cover new UMAP configuration options.

feat: implement inline diff view and processing for pending changes (#262)

* feat: implement inline diff view and processing for pending changes

- Add DiffView component for displaying line-level diffs with added and removed lines.
- Create inlineDiffExtension to highlight changes in the editor with widgets for accepting/rejecting changes.
- Implement readingViewDiffProcessor to highlight changes in reading view and provide action buttons.
- Introduce PendingChangesStore to manage pending changes, including adding, accepting, and rejecting changes.
- Add tests for editNote tool to ensure correct handling of edits, including error cases and sequential edits.

* fix: add edit note tool and enhance pending changes management

- Implemented the edit note tool for targeted search-and-replace edits in markdown notes.
- Updated PendingChangesStore to include cleanup for pending changes on plugin unload.
- Enhanced error handling in edit note tool for various scenarios including non-existent files and ambiguous paths.
- Added tests for the edit note tool to ensure correct functionality and error handling.
- Minor style adjustments in CSS and Svelte components.

* feat: add PendingChangesBar component for managing pending changes in chat

* fix: optimize PendingChangesBar interactions and improve processing group management

* feat: enhance acceptAll functionality to report failed changes in PendingChangesBar and PendingChangesModal

* fix: enhance pending changes management with initial content snapshot and async rejectAll functionality

* fix: update notice messages to use local count variable in handleAcceptAll function

* refactor: remove update_note tool and update related references to edit_note

* Refactor tests and remove deprecated files

- Deleted `helpers.test.ts` and `dataStore.test.ts` as they are no longer needed.
- Updated `registry.test.ts` to replace `getProvider` with `getProviderDefinition` and adjusted related tests accordingly.
- Renamed `createCustomOpenAICompatibleProvider` to `createOpenAICompatibleProvider` in `registry.test.ts`.
- Modified `graphDataBuilder.test.ts` to include additional document vectors and settings for graph building.

* fix: agent model retrieval logic to prevent incorrect values from LLM responses

* fix: implement file locking during revert operation to ensure safe modifications

* Update src/stores/pendingChangesStore.svelte.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add two-pane diff view mode and enhance inline diff functionality

- Introduced a new "two-pane" diff view mode for better visualization of changes in reading view.
- Enhanced inline diff rendering to preserve whitespace and newlines in added text.
- Created a toggle button for switching between "word-diff" and "two-pane" modes in both editor and reading views.
- Updated styles for new diff view components and action bars.
- Refactored reading view diff processor to support new diff modes and improved rendering logic.
- Added settings option for users to select their preferred diff view mode.
- Ensured compatibility with existing pending changes store and data management.

* feat(inlineDiff): improve initialization of decorations and handle refresh events

* feat: add JavaScript execution tool and manage notes functionality

- Implemented executeJavaScript tool with worker support for executing JavaScript snippets and capturing console output.
- Added shared types for JavaScript execution results and payloads.
- Created manageNotes tool for batch operations on markdown notes, including create, update, delete, and move functionalities.
- Enhanced test coverage for executeJavaScript and manageNotes tools, ensuring proper handling of operations and error cases.
- Introduced new utility functions for normalizing note references and validating file operations.

---------

- Refactored MiniSearchService and HNSWVectorStore to accommodate vault ID in database naming.
- Removed deprecated force layout setting from GraphSettings.
- Added comprehensive tests for new dimensionality reduction functions.

fix(searchNotes): improve searchNotes tool configuration and logging

- Reorganized imports for better clarity and consistency.
- Enhanced the searchNotes tool configuration retrieval to accommodate dynamic agent settings.
- Added debug logging statements to track the usage of the Omnisearch plugin and selected search algorithm, improving traceability during searches.

refactor: clean up modal constructors and svelte state usage (#241)

Consolidate multiline constructor arguments for modal classes to single-line
format in TypeScript files to improve readability and reduce diff noise.
Capture initial props explicitly in ToolConfigModal Svelte component so modal
components treat prop values as stable at creation time (avoid Svelte 5
state_referenced_locally warnings). Replace a derived getter with an explicit
initialToolConfig and defaultConfig, and keep update logic intact.

Also tidy various Svelte expression formatting: fix trailing commas and
parens in $derived calls, normalize object literal indentation when creating
modal instances, and extract plugin extension/tool config accessor objects
into clearer blocks. Add AGENTS.md guidance for Svelte 5 $state usage with
examples for modals and reactive components.

readme_html:
Your Smart Second Brain

Your Smart Second Brain is a free and open-source Obsidian plugin to improve your overall knowledge management. It serves as your personal assistant, powered by large language models like ChatGPT or Llama2. It can directly access and process your notes, eliminating the need for manual prompt editing and it can operate completely offline, ensuring your data remains private and secure.

🌟 Features

📝 Chat with your Notes

  • RAG pipeline: All your notes will be embedded into vectors and then retrieved based on the similarity to your query in order to generate an answer based on the retrieved notes
  • Get reference links to notes: Because the answers are generated based on your retrieved notes we can trace where the information comes from and reference the origin of the knowledge in the answers as Obsidian links
  • Chat with LLM: You can disable the function to answer queries based on your notes and then all the answers generated are based on the chosen LLM’s training knowledge
  • Save chats: You can save your chats and continue the conversation at a later time
  • Different chat views: You can choose between two chat views: the ‘comfy’ and the ‘compact’ view

🤖 Choose ANY preferred Large Language Model (LLM)

  • Ollama to integrate LLMs: Ollama is a tool to run LLMs locally. Its usage is similar to Docker, but it's specifically designed for LLMs. You can use it as an interactive shell, through its REST API, or using it from a Python library.
  • Quickly switch between LLMs: Comfortably change between different LLMs for different purposes, for example changing from one for scientific writing to one for persuasive writing.
  • Use ChatGPT: Although, our focus lies on a privacy-focused AI Assistant you can still leverage OpenAI’s models and their advanced capabilities.

⚠️ Limitations

  • Performance depends on the chosen LLM: As LLMs are trained for different tasks, LLMs perform better or worse in embedding notes or generating answers. You can go with our recommendations or find your own best fit.
  • Quality depends on knowledge structure and organization: The response improves when you have a clear structure and do not mix unrelated information or connect unrelated notes. Therefore, we recommend a well-structured vault and notes.
  • AI Assistant might generate incorrect or irrelevant answers: Due to a lack of relevant notes or limitations of AI understanding the AI Assistant might generate unsatisfying answers. In those cases, we recommend rephrasing your query or describing the context in more detail

🔧 Getting started

[!NOTE]
If you use Obsidian Sync the vector store binaries might take up a lot of space due to the version history.
Exclude the .obsidian/plugins/smart-second-brain/vectorstores folder in the Obsidian Sync settings to avoid this.

Follow the onboarding instructions provided on initial plugin startup in Obsidian.

⚙️ Under the hood

Check out our Architecture Wiki page.

🎯 Roadmap

  • Support Gemini and Claude models and integrate KoboldCpp
  • Similar note connections view
  • Chat Threads
  • Hybrid Vector Search
  • Predictive Note Placement
  • Agent with Obsidian tooling
  • Multimodality

🧑‍💻 About us

We initially made this plugin as part of a university project, which is now complete. However, we are still fully committed to developing and improving the assistant in our spare time. This and the papa-ts (backend) repo serve as an experimental playground, allowing us to explore state-of-the-art AI topics further and as a tool to enrich the obsidian experience we’re so passionate about. If you have any suggestions or wish to contribute, we would greatly appreciate it.

📢 You want to support?

  • Report issues or open a feature request here
  • Open a PR for code contributions (Development setup instructions TBD)

❓ FAQ

Don't hesitate to ask your question in the Q&A

Are any queries sent to the cloud?

The queries are sent to the cloud only if you choose to use OpenAI's models. You can also choose Ollama to run your models locally. Therefore, your data will never be sent to any cloud services and stay on your machine.

How does it differ from the SmartConnections plugin?

Our plugin is quite similar to Smart Connections. However, we improve it based on our experience and the research we do for the university.

For now, these are the main differences:

  • We are completely open-source
  • We support Ollama/local models without needing a license
  • We place more value on UI/UX
  • We use a different tech stack leveraging Langchain and Orama as our vector store
  • Under the hood, our RAG pipeline uses other techniques to process your notes like hierarchical tree summarization

What models do you recommend?

OpenAI's models are still the most capable. Especially "GPT-4" and "text-embedding-3-large". The best working local embedding modal we tested so far would be "mxbai-embed-large".

Does it support multi-language vaults?

It’s supported, although the response quality may vary depending on which prompt language is used internally (we will support more translations in the future) and which models you use. It should work best with OpenAI's "text-embedding-large-3" model.
